content: The half-life of Carbon-14 is approximately 5,730 years. This means that every 5,730 years, the amount of Carbon-14 in a sample will decrease by half due to radioactive decay.
raw_content: The half-life of Carbon-14 is approximately 5,730 years. This means that every 5,730 years, the amount of Carbon-14 in a sample will decrease by half due to radioactive decay.
